chapter_title.ar: باب فَضْلِ ارْتِبَاطِ الْخَيْلِ وَ إِجْرَائِهَا وَ الرَّمْيِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 22 – Merits of bridling the cavalry horses and injuring them, and the throwing (Archery)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/390)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/390)
text: لَا سَبَقَ إِلَّا فِي خُفٍّ أَوْ حَافِرٍ أَوْ نَصْلٍ يَعْنِي النِّضَالَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umey, from Hafs,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There shall be no (coming) first except in (racing) a camel-hoof, or a horse hoof, or an arrow-head, meaning archery’.
en.sarwar: 13. Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ahmad from Ali ibn ‘Isma’il in a marfu’ manner the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘You should learn horse riding and sharp-shooting. I like your learning sharp-shooting more than your horse riding.’ He then said, ‘All playing of believers’ is falsehood except three things: horse training, sharp-shooting from his bow and playing with his wife because they are right. However, Allah, the most Majestic, the most Glorious, with the arrow sends three to paradise: one who makes the arrow, one who provides support with it for the cause of Allah and one who shoots it for the cause of Allah.’”
